name: Ami Bera, M.D.
tweet: Before #COVID19 hit, the @CDC warned that *70%* of countries worldwide were not prepared to prevent, detect, and respond to a public health emergency. ⚕️  Many nations just aren’t equipped to deal with the #coronavirus or any future pandemic.  #USGlobalResponse

name: Ami Bera, M.D.

name: Ami Bera, M.D.
tweet: The #COVID19 pandemic has reinforced this. Since originating in China in Dec. 2019, the virus has quickly spread to 170 countries with over 200,000 deaths worldwide.  The virus has hit the U.S. especially hard, with over 50,000 deaths and significant strain on our health systems.

name: Ami Bera, M.D.
tweet: In our interconnected world, it can take only 36 hours for a virus to travel from a remote village to all six continents.  The sooner we act to contain a public health emergency abroad, the safer our citizens are back home.  #USGlobalResponse

name: Ami Bera, M.D.

tweet: First responders can’t choose who they are responding to, or know ahead of time if the individual is carrying #coronavirus or not, so they must now grapple with the constant potential exposure to the virus on a daily basis.

name: Ami Bera, M.D.
tweet: But our first responders also contend with higher mental health stress and higher risk for suicide than other professions.  And the stress imposed by the #coronavirus pandemic is only increasing those burdens.

tweet: They worried that they’d have to pay for their medications with credit cards, piling up debts that’d place their whole retirement in jeopardy. 6/

name: Ami Bera, M.D.
tweet: He called his insurance agent repeatedly, and was assured he’d paid in full. 3/

name: Ami Bera, M.D.
tweet: They paid for a private insurance plan to supplement their Medicare coverage. But Rob began to receive notices from his insurance company claiming that he owed them money, even though he had set up automatic payments. 2/
